key_insight: A linearly implicit mass-lumped projection FEM can enforce the unit-sphere constraint exactly at nodes while delivering unconditional stability and optimal error rates for harmonic map heat flow.
engineering_relevance: The method offers a practical, mesh-efficient way to simulate constrained geometric evolution problems with provable energy decay and no time-step restriction.
method: Nodal projection to restore unit length exactly
Mass-lumped discrete inner product with tensor-product energy nonexpansiveness
evidence_signal: The paper proves unconditional error bounds of O(Δt+h^2) in L∞(0,T;L2) and O(Δt+h) in L2(0,T;H1), and reports numerical experiments matching these rates and energy decay.
watch_item: The strongest discrete energy-dissipation guarantee relies on Cartesian rectangular/cuboidal tensor-product meshes, so implementation on general unstructured meshes may not inherit the same property.
paper_title: Unconditional Optimal Error Estimates and Energy Stability for a Linearly Implicit Mass-Lumped Projection Finite Element Method for the Harmonic Map Flow
